title: Eckington Mill & Croome Park (Worcs)

main_route.original_wording: Eckington Mill camp-site / Court End Farm Campsite
main_route.excerpt: 5.30. We reached Eckington Mill camp-site. There's a padlock to get in the gate, 1812. Court End Farm Campsite
We swept the floor + set off for Groome Park. Groome Park has the old building, Groome Court, which we did not see inside.

geocode_hint.hint: Croome Park, National Trust, near Severn Stoke, Worcestershire

stops.excerpt: We walked off across the 2 rivers - Tindered. Be let off on the island. Looked at Strensham Church. A lot of family memorials, brasses covered because of bats, Norman tub font, saints' paintings (16).

geocode_hint.hint: St John the Baptist Church, Strensham, Worcestershire

unresolved.issue: Garbled transcription; likely refers to a junction or place name near a Sainsbury factory; not a visited stop but mentioned in conversation with a fellow camper.
unresolved.excerpt: The man near us was a scientist next to Toys 9 us at Junet 2 - the longest [?for] Sainsbury factory making chemical stuff, 1860 phosphate + onwards. He lives in Worcs.

summary: A short two-night weekend trip for Simon, Eve and the dog Tinder to Eckington Mill Campsite (Court End Farm) on the River Avon in Worcestershire, leaving Birmingham on Friday 2 September 2016. The Saturday was spent walking to the medieval Strensham Church and picking blackberries; on Sunday they visited Croome Park (National Trust) and its RAF museum before returning home.
extraction_notes: 'Groome Park' and 'Groome Court' are transcription spellings of Croome Park and Croome Court (National Trust, Worcestershire). The RAF hospital/airfield reference is to Defford Airfield, historically associated with the Croome Park estate. 'Junet 2' is an unresolved garbled phrase in a conversation anecdote; it is not a visited place. Only Simon, Eve and Tinder are named as travellers — no children on this trip. Dates inferred: Friday = 2016-09-02, Saturday = 2016-09-03, Sunday = 2016-09-04.
